metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_2556
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig.5: "Electron micrographs demonstrating the subcellular localization of m2 receptor protein"
- " Electron micrograph of a rare somatic synapse between an unlabeled axon terminal (a) and an m2-immunoreactive neuron (~2). Reaction product is not present postsynaptically but is plentiful outside the region of this synapse (arrows)."
- "D, Junctional complex (arrow) binding two m2-immunoreactive dendrites. The junctional densities appear intensely immunoreactive."
- "F, m2-positive axon terminal (a) and preterminal axon (arrow). Synaptic vesicles appear to be surrounded by reaction product."

- A junctional complex is not a synapse.
- Based on the known transmembrane topology of the protein, the "integral component of ... membrane" term was chosen.. Experimental description: "Twelve young adult male Sprague-Dawley (Harlan) rats were deeply anesthetized ..."

checks.label: OWL Consistency
checks.error_message: No inferences attributed to this model graph in the materializer output. Likely a parse failure or other materializer skip — re-run on this model alone to investigate.
